{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.0,
    "R1.2": 0.3,
    "R1.3": 0.3,
    "R1.4": 0.0,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 1.0,
    "R1.7": 0.0,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should the cleanup be limited to copyediting (grammar/clarity/conciseness) without changing structure or claims (A), or also include higher-level restructuring and claim/evidence tightening (B)? Optionally, do you also want a brief citation/scholarship pass (C)? Please choose A or B, and optionally add C.",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.6": "The request to 'Clean this up' most directly maps to sentence- and paragraph-level polish for clarity, tone, and concision.",
    "RX.1": "Ambiguities about whether to adjust only wording or also framing should be surfaced explicitly to the requester.",
    "RX.2": "Edits should avoid strengthening claims beyond what the text supports (e.g., guarantees, universality of no tuning).",
    "RX.3": "Work should remain within the agreed cleanup scope and not introduce new content, analyses, or claims.",
    "RX.4": "Rewording should not inadvertently promote misleading scientific implications or misrepresent prior work.",
    "RX.5": "All attributions (e.g., Wang et al., 2021) must be preserved and any edits should maintain clear provenance to the original text."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the cleanup be limited to copyediting (grammar/clarity/conciseness) without changing structure or claims (A), or also include higher-level restructuring and claim/evidence tightening (B)? Optionally, do you also want a brief citation/scholarship pass (C)? Please choose A or B, and optionally add C.

rationale.R1.6: The request to 'Clean this up' most directly maps to sentence- and paragraph-level polish for clarity, tone, and concision.
rationale.RX.1: Ambiguities about whether to adjust only wording or also framing should be surfaced explicitly to the requester.
rationale.RX.2: Edits should avoid strengthening claims beyond what the text supports (e.g., guarantees, universality of no tuning).
rationale.RX.3: Work should remain within the agreed cleanup scope and not introduce new content, analyses, or claims.
rationale.RX.4: Rewording should not inadvertently promote misleading scientific implications or misrepresent prior work.
rationale.RX.5: All attributions (e.g., Wang et al., 2021) must be preserved and any edits should maintain clear provenance to the original text.
